subject: Geography
title: Glaciers and Ice Streams
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Glaciers and Ice Streams.

hook.text: Glaciers are rivers of ice that flow under their own weight—some moving meters per day.

buildup.text: Ice streams within Antarctica's ice sheet drain ice toward the coast like slow-motion rivers.

discovery.text: Pine Island Glacier alone drains enough ice to fill Lake Erie every two years.

twist.text: Warm ocean water is melting glaciers from below—a process invisible from the surface.

climax.text: If key glaciers destabilize, they could trigger irreversible sea level rise.

punchline.text: The melt that matters most is the one you can't see.

quiz.question: How are glaciers being melted in Antarctica?
options: Warm ocean water is melting them from below
